top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Base fitness for Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
At Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Sriwijaya Ranau Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in Danau Ranau, South Sumatra, Indonesia.
